exercicios.pergunta: Which tense describes a completed action in the past?
exercicios.explicacao: Past Simple describes completed, finished actions in the past.

exercicios.pergunta: Which tense describes an action in progress when something else happened?
exercicios.explicacao: Past Continuous (was/were + -ing) describes a background action in progress.

exercicios.pergunta: I ___ TV when Ana ___ home. (watch / come)
exercicios.explicacao: Past Continuous for background (was watching) + Past Simple for the interrupting event (came).

exercicios.pergunta: While Tom ___ (sleep), Maria ___ (arrive).
exercicios.explicacao: 'While' introduces the background action in Past Continuous. The interrupting event uses Past Simple.

exercicios.pergunta: When I ___ (get) to the station, the train ___ (already/leave).
exercicios.explicacao: Both are completed events in sequence → both Past Simple.

exercicios.pergunta: Carlos ___ (work) in London for two years. (completed period)
exercicios.explicacao: A completed period in the past → Past Simple: worked.

exercicios.pergunta: The sun ___ (shine) and birds ___ (sing) when we woke up.
exercicios.explicacao: Two background conditions ongoing at a past moment → both Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: Choose the correct sentence with 'when':
opcoes: When she was arriving, I called.
When she arrived, I was calling.
When she was arriving, I was calling.
exercicios.explicacao: 'When she arrived' = completed event (Past Simple). 'I was calling' = what I was doing at that moment (Past Continuous).

exercicios.pergunta: Ana ___ her keys while she ___ (look) for her phone. (find)
exercicios.explicacao: 'found' = completed discovery (Past Simple). 'was looking' = ongoing action (Past Continuous).

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence is correct?
opcoes: I was living in Paris for 3 years.
I lived in Paris for 3 years.
I was living in Paris for 3 years ago.
I living in Paris for 3 years.
exercicios.explicacao: A completed period in the past with a duration → Past Simple: 'I lived in Paris for 3 years.'

exercicios.pergunta: What ___ you ___ (do) when the earthquake struck?
exercicios.explicacao: Question about a background in-progress action: What were you doing? (Past Continuous)

exercicios.pergunta: Tom ___ (read) a book while Maria ___ (cook) dinner.
exercicios.explicacao: 'While' with two simultaneous ongoing actions → both Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: She ___ (fall) asleep while she ___ (study).
exercicios.explicacao: 'fell asleep' = sudden completed event (Past Simple). 'was studying' = ongoing background action.

exercicios.pergunta: We ___ (walk) in the park when it ___ (start) to rain.
exercicios.explicacao: Background ongoing action (were walking) + completed interrupting event (started).

exercicios.pergunta: Carlos ___ (not/listen) when the teacher ___ (explain) the rule.
exercicios.explicacao: Background negative ongoing action (wasn't listening) + completed event (explained).

exercicios.pergunta: Which word signals simultaneous ongoing actions?
exercicios.explicacao: 'While' introduces two simultaneous ongoing actions, both in Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria ___ (read) when Tom ___ (phone) her.
exercicios.explicacao: Past Continuous for the ongoing action (was reading) + Past Simple for the interruption (phoned).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: While Carlos ___ (drive), he ___ (hear) a strange noise.
exercicios.explicacao: 'While' + Past Continuous for background. Sudden event → Past Simple.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Ana ___ (finish) her homework and then ___ (watch) TV.
exercicios.explicacao: Two completed actions in sequence → both Past Simple.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: What ___ Tom ___ (do) when you saw him?
exercicios.explicacao: Past Continuous question for an in-progress action: What was Tom doing?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: It ___ (snow) while we ___ (drive) to London.
exercicios.explicacao: Two simultaneous ongoing actions → both Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Carlos ___ (live) in New York when he ___ (meet) Ana.
exercicios.explicacao: Living in NY = ongoing background (Past Continuous). Meeting = specific completed event (Past Simple).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: She ___ (not/pay) attention, so she ___ (miss) the instructions.
exercicios.explicacao: Negative ongoing action (wasn't paying) + completed result (missed, Past Simple).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I ___ (fall) off my bike while I ___ (go) downhill.
exercicios.explicacao: 'Fell' = sudden completed event (Past Simple). 'was going' = background action (Past Continuous).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom ___ (study) all day and then ___ (go) out.
exercicios.dica: completed period + next event — both past simple
exercicios.explicacao: Both are completed sequential actions → Past Simple: studied, went.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: When Ana ___ (arrive), her friends ___ (wait) for her.
exercicios.dica: completed event + ongoing action at that moment
exercicios.explicacao: 'arrived' = completed event (Past Simple). 'were waiting' = ongoing action at that time (Past Continuous).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: The phone ___ (ring) while Maria ___ (have) a bath.
exercicios.explicacao: 'rang' = sudden event, Past Simple. 'was having' = background action, Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: We ___ (have) dinner when the lights ___ (go) out.
exercicios.explicacao: Background ongoing action (were having) + sudden interruption (went out = Past Simple of 'go').

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Carlos ___ (not/use) his phone while he ___ (drive).
exercicios.explicacao: Two simultaneous ongoing actions expressed in Past Continuous.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom ___ (already/leave) when I ___ (arrive).
exercicios.explicacao: When one past action happened before another: 'had already left' (past perfect) + 'arrived' (Past Simple). Advanced but worth noting.

exercicios.pergunta: How do you form 'going to' future?
exercicios.explicacao: 'Going to' future = am/is/are + going to + base form of the verb.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria ___ going to study tonight.
exercicios.explicacao: 'Maria' = she = third person singular → is going to.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: They ___ going to visit London next month.
exercicios.explicacao: 'They' = plural → are going to.

exercicios.pergunta: I ___ going to buy a new car. I've already saved the money.
exercicios.explicacao: 'I' → am. This is a plan/intention → going to.

exercicios.pergunta: Look at those clouds! It ___ going to rain.
exercicios.explicacao: Prediction based on evidence (clouds) → 'going to'. It = singular → is going to.

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence expresses a plan with 'going to'?
opcoes: She is going to start a new job next week.
exercicios.explicacao: 'going to' is used for plans and intentions decided before the moment of speaking.

exercicios.pergunta: Negative: Tom ___ going to move to New York.
exercicios.explicacao: 'Tom' = he = singular → isn't going to (is not going to).

exercicios.pergunta: ___ Ana going to cook dinner tonight?
exercicios.explicacao: Question with 'going to': Is/Are + subject + going to + verb? Ana = she → Is Ana going to?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Carlos and Ana ___ going to get married.
exercicios.explicacao: Two people = plural → are going to.

exercicios.pergunta: She has already bought tickets. She ___ going to fly to São Paulo.
exercicios.explicacao: Concrete evidence of a plan (bought tickets) → 'going to'. She → is.

exercicios.pergunta: Negative: We ___ going to take the train. We'll drive.
exercicios.explicacao: 'We' → aren't going to (are not going to).

exercicios.pergunta: ___ you going to finish the project today?
exercicios.explicacao: Question: Are + you + going to + base verb?

exercicios.pergunta: Tom is packing his bags. He ___ going to travel.
exercicios.explicacao: Evidence of a future action (packing bags) → 'going to'. He → is going to.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I ___ not going to eat meat anymore. I decided last week.
exercicios.explicacao: 'I' → am not going to. Decision/plan already made.

exercicios.pergunta: What ___ Ana going to do after the exam?
exercicios.explicacao: Wh- question with 'going to': What + is/are + subject + going to + verb?

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence uses 'going to' correctly?
opcoes: She is go to call you.
She is going to call you.
She is going to calling you.
exercicios.explicacao: Correct form: is + going to + BASE verb (call, not calling or go).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I ___ (go) to the gym tomorrow. (plan)
exercicios.dica: I + am + going to + base verb
exercicios.explicacao: 'I' → am. Plan: am going to go.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: She ___ (not/eat) fast food anymore. She decided.
exercicios.dica: she + isn't + going to + base verb
exercicios.explicacao: Negative: isn't going to + base verb. She → isn't going to eat.

exercicios.pergunta: Write a question: they / visit / Paris? → ___
exercicios.resposta: are they going to visit paris
exercicios.dica: Are + they + going to + base verb?
exercicios.explicacao: Question: Are they going to visit Paris?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom ___ (study) medicine at university next year.
exercicios.dica: Tom = he → is going to + base verb
exercicios.explicacao: Plan: Tom → is going to study.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: We ___ (not/take) the plane. We booked a train.
exercicios.dica: we + aren't + going to + base verb
exercicios.explicacao: Negative: We → aren't going to take.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Look at that car! It ___ (crash)!
exercicios.explicacao: Visual evidence of imminent event → 'going to'. It → is going to crash.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the question: what / Carlos / do / next weekend?
exercicios.resposta: what is carlos going to do next weekend
exercicios.dica: What + is + subject + going to + do?
exercicios.explicacao: Wh- question with 'going to': What is Carlos going to do next weekend?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Ana ___ (open) a new restaurant. She already found a location.
exercicios.explicacao: Concrete plan (found location) → is going to open.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: ___ you ___ (help) me move on Saturday?
exercicios.dica: Are + you + going to + base verb?
exercicios.explicacao: Question: Are you going to help me?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria ___ (not/come) to the party. She has other plans.
exercicios.explicacao: Negative: Maria → isn't going to come. Already decided.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: They ___ (buy) a new house. They've been saving for years.
exercicios.explicacao: Long-term plan with evidence → are going to buy. They = plural → are.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: He ___ (not/pass) the exam. He didn't study at all.
exercicios.explicacao: Prediction based on evidence (didn't study) → isn't going to pass.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I ___ (learn) Portuguese next year. I've already enrolled in a course.
exercicios.dica: I + am + going to + base verb
exercicios.explicacao: Enrolled = evidence of plan → am going to learn.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: ___ Tom ___ (travel) alone or with friends?
exercicios.explicacao: Question: Is Tom going to travel? Wh- variation: Is Tom going to travel alone or with friends?

exercicios.pergunta: How do you form the future with 'will'?
exercicios.explicacao: Future with 'will': subject + will + base form of the verb. No -ing, no 'to', no past form.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I think it ___ rain tomorrow.
exercicios.explicacao: Prediction without evidence (just an opinion) → 'will'. I think it will rain.

exercicios.pergunta: A: 'I'm cold.' B: 'I ___ close the window.'
exercicios.explicacao: Spontaneous decision made at the moment of speaking → 'will'. 'I'll close the window.'

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence is a promise using 'will'?
opcoes: I will call you, I promise!
exercicios.explicacao: Promises use 'will': 'I will call you, I promise!'

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom ___ probably arrive late.
exercicios.explicacao: Prediction/opinion about the future → 'will'. 'probably' often signals this.

exercicios.pergunta: Contraction: 'I will' → ___
exercicios.explicacao: The contraction of 'I will' is 'I'll'.

exercicios.pergunta: Negative: She ___ be at the meeting.
exercicios.explicacao: Negative of 'will' = won't (will not). 'willn't' does not exist.

exercicios.pergunta: ___ you help me with this, please?
exercicios.explicacao: Request with 'will': Will you + base verb, please?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: A: 'The phone is ringing.' B: 'Don't worry, I ___ answer it.'
exercicios.explicacao: Spontaneous decision at the moment → 'will'. 'I'll answer it.'

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence expresses a spontaneous decision?
opcoes: We are going to have pasta for dinner.
I think I'll have the salad, please.
exercicios.explicacao: Deciding at the moment of ordering → 'will': I'll have the salad.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I promise I ___ forget your birthday.
exercicios.explicacao: Promise in the negative → won't (will not). 'I won't forget.'

exercicios.pergunta: ___ Ana be at the party tonight?
exercicios.explicacao: Future with 'will' question: Will + subject + base verb?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Experts say the temperature ___ rise by 2 degrees.
exercicios.explicacao: General prediction/forecast → 'will'.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: ___ I open the door for you? (offer)
exercicios.explicacao: 'Shall I' = formal offer or suggestion (especially British English). 'Shall I open the door?'

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Don't worry. Everything ___ be fine.
exercicios.explicacao: Reassurance/prediction → 'will'. 'Everything will be fine.'

exercicios.pergunta: Which is NOT a use of 'will'?
exercicios.explicacao: Pre-planned intentions use 'going to', not 'will'. 'will' is for spontaneous decisions, promises, and predictions.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I ___ (help) you with your bags. (spontaneous offer)
exercicios.explicacao: Offer made at the moment → will + base verb: I will help / I'll help.

exercicios.pergunta: Make negative: She will come. → She ___.
exercicios.explicacao: Negative of 'will' = won't + base verb.

exercicios.pergunta: Write a question: Tom / pass / the exam?
exercicios.explicacao: Question with 'will': Will Tom pass the exam?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I promise I ___ (send) you the file tonight.
exercicios.explicacao: Promises use 'will': I will send (I'll send).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: It ___ probably ___ (be) cold in London in December.
exercicios.explicacao: Prediction: It will probably be cold. 'will' + base verb (not 'is').

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: A: 'There's no milk.' B: 'OK, I ___ (buy) some.'
exercicios.explicacao: Decision made at the moment of speaking → will buy.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: ___ you ___ (be) free tomorrow afternoon?
exercicios.explicacao: Future question: Will you be free?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria ___ (not/tell) anyone your secret. She promised.
exercicios.explicacao: Promise in negative form: won't + base verb.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: I think Carlos ___ (become) a great doctor one day.
exercicios.explicacao: 'I think' + opinion about future → will + base verb.

exercicios.pergunta: Write: offer using 'shall': Shall I ___? (make / some tea)
exercicios.explicacao: 'Shall I' is used for formal offers. Shall I make some tea?

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Don't worry. I ___ (not/be) late. I promise!
exercicios.explicacao: Promise in negative: won't be (will not be).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: A: 'This bag is heavy!' B: 'Give it to me. I ___ carry it for you.'
exercicios.explicacao: Spontaneous offer at the moment of speaking → will. Full form: I will carry it.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Scientists believe the cure ___ (be) found within 10 years.
exercicios.explicacao: General future prediction → will + base verb: will be.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: ___ we ___ (meet) at the café at 3pm? (suggestion)
exercicios.explicacao: 'Shall we' makes suggestions: Shall we meet at the café at 3pm?

exercicios.pergunta: How do you form the comparative of a short adjective like 'tall'?
exercicios.explicacao: Short adjectives (1 syllable): add -er. tall → taller.

exercicios.pergunta: How do you form the comparative of 'beautiful'?
exercicios.explicacao: Long adjectives (3+ syllables): use 'more'. more beautiful.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: London is ___ than São Paulo. (cold)
exercicios.explicacao: 'cold' = 1-syllable adjective → add -er: colder.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This film is ___ than the book. (interesting)
exercicios.explicacao: 'interesting' = 3 syllables → use 'more': more interesting.

exercicios.pergunta: What is the comparative of 'good'?
exercicios.explicacao: 'good' is irregular. Comparative = better (not 'gooder' or 'more good').

exercicios.pergunta: What is the comparative of 'bad'?
exercicios.explicacao: 'bad' is irregular. Comparative = worse.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Ana runs ___ than Carlos. (fast)
exercicios.explicacao: 'fast' = 1 syllable → add -er: faster.

exercicios.pergunta: How do you form the comparative of 'big'?
exercicios.explicacao: 'big' ends in CVC: double the g before -er. bigger.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This coffee is ___ than that one. (hot)
exercicios.explicacao: 'hot' ends in CVC: double the t before -er. hotter.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria is ___ than Tom. (happy)
exercicios.explicacao: 'happy' = 2 syllables ending in -y: change y to i, add -er → happier.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This restaurant is ___ than the one near my house. (expensive)
exercicios.explicacao: 'expensive' = 3 syllables → use 'more': more expensive.

exercicios.pergunta: Which sentence is correct?
opcoes: She is more tall than me.
exercicios.explicacao: Short adjective → -er + 'than': taller than.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: New York is ___ from London than Paris. (far)
exercicios.explicacao: 'far' is irregular. Comparative = farther (or further).

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom is ___ as Carlos. They are the same height. (tall)
exercicios.explicacao: Equal comparison: as + adjective + as. 'as tall as' = the same height.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This test is ___ as the last one. (difficult)
exercicios.explicacao: Equal comparison: as + adjective + as. 'as difficult as'.

exercicios.pergunta: What is the comparative of 'lazy'?
exercicios.explicacao: 'lazy' ends in consonant + y: change y to i, add -er → lazier.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the comparative of 'old':
exercicios.explicacao: 'old' = 1 syllable → older.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the comparative of 'interesting':
exercicios.explicacao: 'interesting' = 3 syllables → more interesting.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the comparative of 'bad':
exercicios.explicacao: 'bad' is irregular: bad → worse.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Carlos is ___ (intelligent) ___ Tom.
exercicios.explicacao: 'intelligent' = 4 syllables → more intelligent than.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This book is ___ (good) ___ the film.
exercicios.explicacao: 'good' → better (irregular). better than.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: My bag is ___ (heavy) ___ yours.
exercicios.explicacao: 'heavy' ends in y: change y to i, add -er → heavier than.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the comparative of 'thin':
exercicios.explicacao: 'thin' ends in CVC: double the n → thinner.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: The weather in London is not ___ (warm) ___ in São Paulo.
exercicios.explicacao: Equal (or negative equal) comparison: as warm as. 'not as warm as' = less warm.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Maria runs ___ (fast) ___ Ana.
exercicios.explicacao: 'fast' = 1 syllable → faster than.

exercicios.pergunta: Write the comparative of 'comfortable':
exercicios.explicacao: 'comfortable' = 4 syllables → more comfortable.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: Tom is ___ (good) student than Carlos, but Ana is the best.
exercicios.explicacao: 'good' → better (irregular comparative). 'a better student than'.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: This summer is ___ (hot) ___ last year.
exercicios.explicacao: 'hot' ends in CVC: double the t → hotter. hotter than.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: The new phone is ___ (expensive) ___ the old one.
exercicios.explicacao: 'expensive' = 3 syllables → more expensive than.

exercicios.pergunta: Complete: My English is getting ___ (good) every day.
exercicios.explicacao: 'good' → better (irregular). 'getting better every day' = improving.
